name: mongodump and mongorestore Workflow
description: Create full and partial MongoDB backups with mongodump and restore them with mongorestore for disaster recovery and data migration.
trendingReason: Backup and restore is a fundamental skill required for every production MongoDB deployment
prerequisites: mongodump and mongorestore in PATH (included with MongoDB Database Tools)

steps.title: Backup an entire database
steps.description: Create a full binary backup of a database with compressed output.
steps.tip: The --gzip flag compresses individual BSON files, reducing backup size by 50-70%.

steps.title: Backup a single collection
steps.description: Dump only a specific collection for targeted backup or migration.

steps.title: Backup with a query filter
steps.description: Export only documents matching a query for selective backup.

steps.title: Restore a full database backup
steps.description: Restore all collections from a backup directory into the target database.
steps.warning: By default mongorestore inserts documents alongside existing data. Use --drop to replace existing collections.

steps.title: Restore with --drop to replace existing data
steps.description: Drop each collection before restoring to get an exact replica of the backup.
steps.warning: The --drop flag deletes all current data in each collection before restoring. Double-check the target database name.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# MongoDB Backup Script
set -euo pipefail

DB_NAME="mydb"
BACKUP_DIR="/backups/mongodb"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
BACKUP_PATH="$BACKUP_DIR/$TIMESTAMP"
mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

echo "=== Starting backup of $DB_NAME ==="
mongodump --db="$DB_NAME" --gzip --out="$BACKUP_PATH"

echo ""
echo "=== Backup contents ==="
ls -lhR "$BACKUP_PATH/$DB_NAME/"

echo ""
echo "=== Backup size ==="
du -sh "$BACKUP_PATH"

echo ""
echo "=== Verifying backup integrity ==="
mongorestore --db="${DB_NAME}_verify" --gzip --dryRun "$BACKUP_PATH/$DB_NAME/" 2>&1 | tail -5

echo ""
echo "=== Cleaning up old backups (30+ days) ==="
find "$BACKUP_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type d -mtime +30 -exec rm -rf {} \; -print

echo ""
echo "=== To restore, run: ==="
echo "mongorestore --db=$DB_NAME --drop --gzip $BACKUP_PATH/$DB_NAME/"

faq.question: mongodump vs filesystem snapshots: which is better?
faq.answer: For large databases (100GB+), filesystem snapshots (LVM, EBS) are faster because they are nearly instant. mongodump is better for smaller databases, selective backups, and cross-version migrations since it produces portable BSON files.

faq.question: Can I backup a running production database?
faq.answer: Yes, mongodump creates a consistent snapshot using an oplog if connected to a replica set (use --oplog flag). For standalone servers, mongodump still works but consistency during heavy writes is not guaranteed.

faq.question: How do I backup a database with authentication?
